title: How AI "Thinks" vs How You Think
concept: AI doesn't think like a human — it does fast pattern-matching and prediction, with no real understanding, memory, or intent.

exercises.prompt: Recap: when did the idea of AI begin?
exercises.explain: The 1950s — AI is a 70-year-old idea, not a 2022 invention.

cards.title: Does AI actually "think"?
cards.body: It feels like it understands you. But under the hood, it's doing something very different from human thought.

cards.title: It turns everything into numbers
cards.body: AI converts your words and images into numbers, then finds patterns in them. No mental pictures, no meaning — just math.

cards.title: Super-autocomplete
cards.body: A chatbot predicts the next most-likely word, over and over — like your phone's autocomplete on steroids. That's prediction, not reasoning.

cards.title: No understanding, no memory
cards.body: It doesn't "know" facts or remember you between chats (unless it's given memory). It has no feelings, beliefs, or intentions.

cards.title: Why it can be confidently wrong
cards.body: Because it predicts plausible-sounding text, it can state false things with total confidence — we'll cover these "hallucinations" later.

exercises.prompt: How does a chatbot like ChatGPT mainly produce an answer?

options.text: By predicting the next most-likely word
By looking it up in a database of answers

exercises.explain: It generates text one predicted word at a time — powerful pattern prediction, not understanding.

exercises.prompt: True or false: AI understands the meaning of your words the way a human does.
exercises.explain: False. It maps words to numbers and patterns — there's no human-style understanding behind it.

exercises.sentence: AI turns words and images into ___ to find patterns.
exercises.explain: Everything becomes numbers first — that's how the math of pattern-matching works.

exercises.prompt: Why can AI sound confident but be totally wrong?

options.text: It predicts plausible-sounding text, not verified truth

exercises.explain: It optimizes for text that sounds right — which isn't the same as being right. Always fact-check.

exercises.prompt: Which best describes how today's AI "thinks"?

options.text: Fast pattern-matching and prediction — no real understanding
It memorizes the whole internet word-for-word

exercises.explain: Exactly — it's prediction at massive scale. Useful and impressive, but not human-style thinking.
